zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Beauchamp於1322年3月25日出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾時，她的父親Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset17歲，母親Margaret Porter18歲。她於1363年與Thomas John Bedlisgate在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1384年3月在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Beauchamp于1322年3月25日出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾时，她的父亲Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset17岁，母亲Margaret Porter18岁。她于1363年与Thomas John Bedlisgate在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1384年3月在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Beauchamp was born on 25 March 1322, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, was 17 and her mother, Margaret Porter, was 18. She married Thomas John Bedlisgate in 1363, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in March 1384, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Beauchamp est née le 25 mars 1322, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Margaret Porter, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas John Bedlisgate en 1363, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en mars 1384, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Beauchamp am 25. März 1322 in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset 17 und ihre Mutter Margaret Porter 18. Sie hat 1363 in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas John Bedlisgate geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb im März 1384 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beauchamp è nata il il 25 marzo 1322 nel Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Margaret Porter, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Thomas John Bedlisgate nel 1363, nel Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel marzo 1384, nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Worcester, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Beauchampが1322年3月25日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somersetは17歳，母Margaret Porterは18歳であった。1363年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアでThomas John Bedlisgateと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1384年3月にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Beauchamp(이)가 1322년 3월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Porter(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1363년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 Thomas John Bedlisgate(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1384년 3월 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beauchamp nasceu em 25 de março de 1322, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Porter, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas John Bedlisgate em 1363, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em março de 1384, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Beauchamp родилась 25 марта 1322 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Porter, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas John Bedlisgate в 1363 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла марта 1384 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Beauchamp nació el 25 de marzo de 1322, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Beauchamp 2nd Baron Beauchamp of Somerset, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Margaret Porter, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Thomas John Bedlisgate en 1363, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en marzo de 1384, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
